mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for equity valuations, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in >65% of periods despite rising rates.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weak consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of triggered instances or t-stat for return difference vs baseline is < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer spending and construction activity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) or joint positive signals yield negative returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during mid-cycle expansion or out-of-sample predictive accuracy drops below 52% over 20-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation trigger months or inflation threshold fails to predict return sign (AUC < 0.55).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive power when either series moves independently, or 20-day returns are negative in >58% of co-occurring positive labor months.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth rate shows zero correlation with 60-day forward returns or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 0.5 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain stress, forcing negative S&P 500 adjustments over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of contraction trigger months or the threshold fails to separate return distributions (KS test p > 0.05).
